content.problemStatement: Given a string **s**. Can you make it a palindrome by deleting exactly one character? Note that size of the string after deletion would be one less than it was before.
constraints: String s contains lowercase English alphabets (i.e., from 'a' to 'z')
For subtask 2, 2 <= length of string s <= 10^5
Sum of length of string s over all the input test cases won't exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string s.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if it is possible to make the string a palindrome by deleting exactly one character, otherwise print NO.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: For the first test case, deleting any one 'a' results in a palindrome 'aa'. For the second test case, it is not possible to delete exactly one character and get a palindrome. For the third test case, deleting 'c' results in a palindrome 'abdba'. For the fourth test case, deleting 'b' results in a palindrome 'aba'.
Explanation: For the first test case, deleting 'a' results in a palindrome 'b'. For the second test case, deleting 'b' results in a palindrome 'aa'.
Explanation: For this test case, deleting 'c' results in a palindrome 'abba'.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use two pointers, one at the start and one at the end of the string, and move them towards each other. If a mismatch is found, we check if deleting the character at either pointer results in a palindrome. If not, we return NO. Otherwise, we return YES.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total, where N is the length of the string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the pointers and the result.
